satire_attempt: Founding engineer of 4-year-old SaaS startup pivots all-hands deck after staff engineer interruption. New deck title: 'Our Inherited Substrate, In Reverse Chronological Order.' Slide 47: tesserae. Slide 48: a single stone tablet. Q3 OKR set to: 'Reduce ownership-language tokens by 15%.' Industry insiders predict acquihire by Auth0 within eighteen months.
verdict_reason: Mocking the verse requires either (a) inventing the claim that engineers should make Roman tesserae slides — which the analog does not claim — or (b) inventing the claim that audit-your-ownership-language is performative. The analog explicitly couples the language audit to design consequences (Auth0 dependency vs. custom JWT). The falsifiability disclaims the misuse ('the need is ancient' as cover for never improving anything). My satire would only land against a strawman the verse explicitly disclaims. PASS.
